message: [standing-orders] ORCH armed — substantial decomposable work detected. Write an explicit end-to-end GOAL; drive it to done (impl → tests → review → LIVE verification, real UI/browser path when one exists); never report partial progress as done. Parallelize INDEPENDENT lanes only — one subagent per lane with its own goal + done-means; synthesize as lanes return; intervene on drift. Frontier tokens = judgment only; delegate down the ladder (builder/quick-fix → GLM-5.2 → local Ollama, free). At commitment boundaries consult a strongest OTHER-vendor frontier advisor (skills/_shared/model_roster.py pick_panel — Claude host → GPT via codex; GPT host → strongest Claude via claude CLI/MCP). Full protocol: team-lead · ORCHESTRATION.md §6 · loop-engineering before any fan-out.
_note: ORCH tier. Absorbs loop-engineering's fleet-orchestration-intent probe content verbatim (the explicit spawn/dispatch/parallel-agents block, unmodified except this file's own wrapping) plus decomposable-work signals: build/migrate/refactor/port-family verbs (some object- or preposition-guarded to dodge noun-only uses like 'the running build'), move/convert/replace/break-dependency patterns, a write+object-noun pattern, all/entire/whole/every scope, end-to-end, make-tests-pass, ship-feature, multi-conjunct, and test-and-fix. prompt_intent reuses hook.py's detect_user_correction detector, which does NOT read `unless_pattern` (confirmed by reading hook.py: only detect_forbidden_regex checks it) -- an unless_pattern field here would be a silent no-op landmine, so it is omitted. A fixed-width negative lookbehind ((?<!don't )(?<!never )...) guards the highest-risk bare trigger ('use your team') against ADJACENT negation only ('don't use your team'); phrase-separated negation ('no need to use the team') is NOT caught -- accepted residual gap, see SKILL.md HONEST LIMITATIONS. Deliberately does NOT treat bare 'keep going' / 'continue' as ORCH signals -- the holdout fixture includes a none-labeled 'keep going, add another test case' trap proving those words alone are not decomposable-work evidence. Measured: tuning corpus (skills/standing-orders/tools/corpus_tuning.jsonl) ORCH recall 28/28 (orchestrate+both), 0/14 none false-fires; adversarial holdout (tools/corpus_holdout.jsonl, deliberately varied vocabulary + 'no subagents'/'don't'/'keep going' red herrings) ORCH recall 28/28, 0/14 none false-fires.

message: [standing-orders] DEEP armed — high-level task. BEFORE committing to an approach, run a blindspot pass: what questions should be asked, what does good look like, what prior/historical work exists, which known potholes apply. DURING work, collect lesson candidates — corrections AND confirmed approaches, with why they mattered. At closeout persist only VERIFIED lessons: one lesson per file, one-line summary at top; update existing notes rather than duplicate; delete notes proven wrong. Gate via write-gate → wiki-memory.
_note: DEEP tier. Covers architecture/strategy/tradeoff/roadmap/postmortem/retrospective/unfamiliar-domain seeds, a bare-verb set (draft/whiteboard/survey/analyze/propose/decide-between/define-scoped/rethink-family/figure-out/best-approach), a 'how should/do/to (i|we)? PLANVERB' approach-question form restricted to a planning-verb list (deliberately excludes generic 'how do you <verb>' factual lookups -- 'how do i exit vim' is a holdout none-labeled trap that a naive bare-subject version of this pattern used to false-fire on), an article-optional design+object architecture pattern, and a loose 'X vs Y' comparison trigger (intentionally broad -- a false fire here only adds an unwanted blindspot-pass nudge, never blocks or auto-acts). Measured: tuning corpus DEEP recall 27/28 (deep+both) -- one accepted miss ('what db access pattern fits this data model', a bare implicit architecture question with no strategy/approach marker word), 0/14 none false-fires; adversarial holdout DEEP recall 28/28, 0/14 none false-fires.
